game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Hyperstone Heist

danger_signals.visual_form: Airwave takeover, disruption of normal communication
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of control, imminent threat to world stability

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental distortion, scale transformation of recognizable landmarks
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural threat, reality-warping danger from Dimension X

danger_signals.visual_form: Flash of light, sudden environmental change
Faster enemy movement patterns, increased attack frequency
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat level, heightened combat danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Four distinct playable TMNT characters with signature colors (green with individual equipment)
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and protection; heroes are present
Strength through alliance; safety in numbers

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: New York City as home base/starting location

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types: Leatherhead, Rocksteady, Tatsu, Baxter Stockman, Krang, Super Shredder (progressive power escalation)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Shredder's Hideout (level 3), The Gauntlet (level 4), associated with Dimension X artifacts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: April O'Neil reporting normally from Liberty Island
Sudden flash of light, Manhattan Island begins shrinking, airwaves hijacked
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced hero response; Turtles mobilize to pursue Shredder

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progression from New York City → Ghost Ship → Shredder's Hideout → Gauntlet → Final confrontation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy AI becomes more aggressive; boss difficulty increases; faster-moving gameplay across levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hyperstone artifact, supernatural effects, reality distortion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Introduction of otherworldly enemies (Krang), dimension-specific environmental hazards

environmental_storytelling.example: Five levels progress from familiar (New York City) to hostile (Ghost Ship, Hideout) to final confrontation spaces (Gauntlet, Final Shell Shock)

environmental_storytelling.technique: Visual contrast between civilian and villain spaces
environmental_storytelling.example: Liberty Island and NYC represent normal world; Shredder's Hideout and The Gauntlet visually communicate enemy-controlled territory
Six distinct bosses (Leatherhead, Rocksteady, Tatsu, Baxter Stockman, Krang, Super Shredder) suggest organizational hierarchy and multiple threat types within Shredder's forces
Praised character animation communicates a coherent, believable world despite 16-bit limitations

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Faster movement patterns, increased attack frequency compared to Turtles in Time version
Progresses from broadcast announcer to final boss (Super Shredder form), indicating growing danger
Dash ability assignment, active pursuit of Shredder across five distinct level locations
Appears as boss, representing Dimension X threat manifestation

notes: This beat 'em up communicates world-state primarily through level geography, enemy progression, and visual escalation rather than explicit UI elements. The game uses spatial storytelling (moving from NYC to Shredder's domain) and character animation to establish threat levels. Political situation is simplified: clear hero/villain binary with Shredder as autocratic threat to world order. Dimension X serves as external threat metaphor. Character animation quality ('some of the best you'll ever see') enhances believability of the threat world.
